personal.name: Archit Pai
personal.title: Full-Stack Engineer | Maps, Backend & Cloud
personal.tagline: Building scalable systems through steady experimentation

hobbies.title: Gym & Fitness
hobbies.narrative: Started training to be the next Mr. Olympia. Now I'm addicted to the pain. Send help.
hobbies.details: Current split: Push/Pull/Legs, 6 days a week. PR deadlift: 315 lbs. Still can't do a muscle-up though. Working on it.

hobbies.title: Local LLM Experiments
hobbies.narrative: Running LLMs locally and experimenting with fine-tuning, RAG pipelines, and my own "Claude-like" coding assistant. Pretty sure my M4 is plotting revenge at this point.
hobbies.details: Stack: Ollama + llama.cpp on M4 Pro. Built a RAG pipeline over my own codebase. Fine-tuned a 7B model on geospatial Q&A. Inference at 40 tok/s.

hobbies.title: Autonomous Agent Tinkering
hobbies.narrative: Obsessed with fully autonomous agents like OpenClaw, ZeroClaw, and Hermes Agents. Currently building simple versions for very specific goals. Next on the list: world domination. (Just kidding... unless?)
hobbies.details: Building task-specific agents with tool use and memory. Exploring Hermes Agents for multi-agent orchestration. Current project: an agent that monitors deployments and auto-fixes common failures. Uses Claude as the brain.

experiences.diaryNarrative: Finally got promoted. Mom was so proud she called all the relatives. All of them. My phone didn't stop ringing for a week. Worth it.
resumeBulletPoints: First engineering hire; helped build and scale the company's core geospatial product stack from early implementation to production across map rendering, routing, analytics, positioning, and data pipelines
Architected and owned the hybrid map tile delivery system, balancing real-time updates, database load, and infrastructure cost for high-density geospatial datasets

Drove the implementation from static to dynamic to hybrid tile generation, benchmarking each approach and pivoting mid-implementation to a cache-layered architecture to avoid long-term scalability failures
Designed trigger-based tile regeneration and cache invalidation across cold storage and Redis hot cache, enabling near-real-time updates while preventing low-zoom regeneration cascades
Solved low-zoom performance bottlenecks involving millions of features by introducing category-aware zoom visibility and locality-based invalidation strategies
Served as technical lead for the tile pipeline, defining architectural conventions, reviewing PRs, and mentoring two mid-level engineers end-to-end

experiences.diaryNarrative: Discovered the magic of PostGIS. Spent way too many late nights debugging polygons with holes in all the wrong places. Geometry broke me before I learned to tame it.
resumeBulletPoints: Founding member of a new geospatial platform team, building systems from early proof-of-concept to production deployments serving enterprise clients
Designed and implemented core GIS infrastructure using PostgreSQL/PostGIS, vector tiles, and OSM data, building strong practical experience in spatial data modeling and querying
Built end-to-end web mapping applications delivering POI search, indoor wayfinding, and location data management
Transitioned toward backend and infrastructure ownership as the team scaled, contributing to API design, database architecture, and Azure deployments while reviewing frontend contributions
Built key parts of an indoor geofencing system for a major automotive client PoC under production-grade timelines and constraints

experiences.diaryNarrative: Day 1: Broke production. Day 2: Fixed it. Day 3–365: Pretended Day 1 never happened.
resumeBulletPoints: Built and maintained multiple internal and customer-facing location-based web applications across frontend, backend, and data layers
Implemented mapping features including POI search, wayfinding, and user location storage for GPS and indoor positioning using a proprietary SDK
Developed analytics dashboards visualizing real-time and historical user movement data for monitoring, analysis, and planning
Contributed to nationwide population coverage visualization tools to assess service reach and inform rollout planning
Built supporting tools including client-facing SDK integrations, BLE beacon configuration interfaces, and indoor positioning prototypes using Wi-Fi and Raspberry Pi
Migrated systems from script-heavy monoliths to state-managed React frontends and Azure Functions-based microservice backends

experiences.diaryNarrative: On day one as an intern, they gave me real data to work with. I spent 40% of the time building dashboards and 60% perfecting my "focused face" whenever the boss walked by.
resumeBulletPoints: Built an interactive incident analysis dashboard for maritime operations, visualizing vessel-level safety incidents, mechanical failures, and mitigation actions
Performed exploratory data analysis using Python (pandas, matplotlib) to identify failure patterns from historical incident and maintenance datasets
Designed a data ingestion pipeline and implemented an early predictive model for mechanical part failures, translating operational data into actionable insights
Developed a React-based frontend with coordinated interactive charts supporting time-range filtering and cross-chart highlighting
